## Use `semantic_grep` to find code

The semantic_grep tool:

- has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase
- enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval)
- includes re-ranker
- it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it!
- while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg`

## Committing

- `git diff` is a sanity check!
- COMMIT when you're done.
- Use `--author="qwen3.6-35b-a3b<wes.mcclure+qwen3.6-35b-a3b@gmail.com>"`


## 🙌  TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS

- 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents
- 🙌 `fd` to search file names

## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS

If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom.

- ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f`

Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions.

## Fish Shell

- Use snake_case for names
- Use long options, i.e. `--quiet` over `-q`, `--regex` over `-r`

Take advantage of fish-isms:
```fish

# use `not`, avoid `!`
if not test -f spoon
    echo There is no spoon
end

# capture command output and react to its success/failure (exit status)
if set some_output (some_command)
    echo "ok: $some_output"
else
    echo "fail: $some_output"
end

# prefer named arguments
function show_variable --argument-names name value
    echo "$name = $value"
end

# fish doesn't have heredocs, use mulit-line strings:
echo "foo
bar" | string join ","

# blocks combine the output of multiple commands:

# every variable is an array

for name in $names

string unescape $escaped

import re

with open('~/.config/fish/config.fish', 'r') as f:

     r'for file in ($1 | string match -v "*.tests.fish")'),

    (r'for file in (\$WES_DOTFILES/fish/load_last_interactive_only/\*\.fish)',
     r'for file in ($1 | string match -v "*.tests.fish")'),
]

for pattern, replacement in patterns:
    content = re.sub(pattern, replacement, content)

with open('~/.config/fish/config.fish', 'w') as f:

print("Done!")
